description: Closed, curated vocabulary of relation types. Every edge in the fact graph MUST use a relation_id from this list. Adding a relation type is a deliberate, documented act - never ad hoc - so the relational layer stays checkable and free of vocabulary drift.
amendment_rule: A new relation type may be added only as an explicit edit to this file, with a definition and an inverse (or 'none'). Edges referencing an unknown relation_id are invalid and must not be published.

relations.definition: The source fact is constructed from, and depends on, the target fact. If the target changes or is withdrawn, the source must be re-evaluated.
The source fact is a foundation that the target depends on. Inverse of derives_from.
The source is a repo artifact (report, receipt) that substantiates the target fact. Every published fact requires at least one evidences edge or an inline evidence reference.
The source fact is substantiated by the target artifact. Inverse of evidences.
The source fact replaces the target fact, which is now historical. Used for status changes (e.g. a 1-34 roadmap fact supersedes the prior 1-31 fact).
The source fact has been replaced by the target fact. Inverse of supersedes.
The source (a site fact) is a faithful public copy of the target (a repo fact). Asserts the site is publishing, not authoring.
The source repo fact is mirrored publicly by the target site fact. Inverse of mirrors.
The source fact's interpretation is constrained by the target boundary statement (e.g. a 'proved' status bounded_by the proved-vs-released boundary).
The source boundary statement constrains the interpretation of the target fact. Inverse of bounded_by.
The source term-node provides the canonical definition for the target term as used in facts.
The source fact or term uses a term whose canonical definition is the target term-node. Inverse of defines.
The source term or fact was first introduced by the target stage, paper, or artifact.
The source stage/paper/artifact first introduced the target term or fact. Inverse of introduced_in.
The source is a conjectured deeper structure proposed to underlie the target, NOT yet proved. Status of such a fact must be 'hypothesis'. Distinct from underlies, which asserts a proved foundation.
